newsSince: On June 2 STMicroelectronics raised its 2026 data-center revenue ambition to ~$1B (from 'nicely above $500M') and flagged 2027 could double, on an extended AWS engagement and NVIDIA-co-developed 800VDC power portfolio; on June 16 it launched a $1.5B dual-tranche convertible bond and announced early redemption of its 2027 zero-coupon convert. Q1 GAAP gross margin was a weak 33.8% with ~100bps Q2 unused-capacity drag. (Note: STM.DE on latent-ai-nodes-strict is Stabilus SE, a different company - FY26 guidance reaffirmed, June 16 SBTi commitment, humanoid-robotics product wins; no re-rate.)

sources.label: STM lifts 2026 data-center revenue target to ~$1B (Globe and Mail)
STM raises DC guidance amid 89% net income drop (ActuIA)

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha-vs-passives lens. June 2 DC revenue doubling to ~$1B is a genuine fundamental upgrade for power/control semis, but the name is increasingly discovered and weak 33.8% gross margin caps quality; small net positive without re-ranking vs secondary residual-alpha peers.
verdicts.bull: DC ambition doubled to ~$1B (2026), 2027 could double again; AWS + NVIDIA 800VDC anchor power-delivery content.
verdicts.neutral: Real AI tailwind offset by depressed margins and a more crowded setup; residual alpha shrinking.
verdicts.bear: 33.8% GM and unused-capacity drag; auto/industrial cyclicality; DC story now consensus.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: ~$1B 2026 data-center revenue ramp via AWS/NVIDIA 800VDC power.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Structurally low gross margin and auto/industrial cycle weakness.

verdicts.rankRationale: This listing is Stabilus SE (STM.DE), not STMicroelectronics. FY26 guidance reaffirmed; June 16 SBTi commitment is immaterial; humanoid-robotics/automation product wins highlighted but no order quantification. Latent motion-control-for-robotics pathway unchanged; hold.
verdicts.bull: Reaffirmed FY26 guide; gripper/actuator portfolio gives optionality on humanoid robotics and automation.
verdicts.neutral: No quantified robotics revenue; thesis remains speculative and latent.
verdicts.bear: Auto/industrial-exposed cyclical; robotics is years from material contribution; soft FCF quarter.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Motion-control/gripper adoption in industrial automation and humanoid robotics.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Cyclical end-markets; robotics optionality may not monetize.

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node-bottleneck lens favors HBM/advanced-packaging/test/networking directness. STM's ~$1B DC power ramp is positive but power/analog is adjacent, not a core node bottleneck; the June upgrade slightly firms relevance without changing its low-tier standing vs direct-node peers.
verdicts.bull: 800VDC power-delivery for AI racks is mission-critical infrastructure; DC revenue doubling validates AI pull.
verdicts.neutral: Power/analog is AI-adjacent, not an advanced-node/packaging bottleneck.
verdicts.bear: Limited direct HBM/CPO/packaging/test exposure; remains a removal candidate on this lens.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Weak direct AI-node bottleneck relevance vs packaging/test peers.

verdicts.rankRationale: Co-packaged-optics lens: STM has limited CPO directness. June DC power news is unrelated to optical interconnect; nothing advances its CPO relevance. Auto/industrial cycle risk persists. Hold low on this page.
verdicts.bull: Quality broad-line semi with growing AI power-delivery content.
verdicts.neutral: No CPO/optical exposure; June news is power-side, not interconnect.
verdicts.bear: Minimal co-packaged-optics linkage; cyclical auto/industrial drag and soft margins.

verdicts.rankRationale: Under-owned-bottleneck master list, board-level power & passives bucket. June 2 doubling of DC revenue ambition is a real positive for power-delivery content, but the bottleneck is no longer hidden and margins are weak; small positive drift, no peer re-rank.
verdicts.bull: AI-server PDN/power-semi content scaling with ~$1B 2026 DC revenue; NVIDIA/AWS design pull.
verdicts.neutral: Exposure confirmed but increasingly discovered; quality capped by margin.
verdicts.bear: Low gross margin, cyclical auto/industrial base, crowding on the AI-power narrative.
